channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SURPLUS REVENUES FUEL INCOME TAX CUTS
item.description: (May 2026) One sentence in an annual state report explains how policymakers have achieved a serious fiscal goal: reduction of the top state individual income tax rate under three governors and a succession of Republican legislatures. “(Arkansas) revenues are expanding because the business cycle’s current state is expansion, not contraction.” Policy Foundation research memo, Nov. […]

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] TOP ARKANSAS INCOME TAX RATE CUT TO 3.7%
item.description: (May 2026) Arkansas policymakers reduced the top state individual income tax rate from 3.9% to 3.7% in early May, using an estimated $334.4 million projected fiscal surplus in the current 2025-26 fiscal year. Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders called a special session of the state legislature April 30 to consider the tax cut. The session convened […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend
item.description:
Granite Staters can expect to have the most affordable Memorial Day Weekend in New England this year, as New Hampshire offers the region’s lowest gas and grocery prices, a Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy review of regional prices shows.  Memorial Day weekend kicks off both summer travel season and outdoor grilling season, and this […]

The post NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Empowerms] Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again?
item.description:
Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? On this episode of the Empower Podcast, State Rep. Otis Anthony joins Grant for a wide-ranging conversation about poverty, work, education, economic development, and the future of the Mississippi Delta. Anthony shares how growing up in Indianola shaped his view of public service, why he believes the Delta’s…

The post Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Libertas] Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families
item.description:
Libertas Network’s acquisition of Praxis ignites a bold revolution in education by empowering young innovators to bypass outdated systems and chart their own path to success.

The post Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying
item.description:
We hear it all too often from In-Home Support Services (IHSS) care providers considering opting out of their union: “If I do, I’ll lose my dental insurance.”

The post CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] AICOA Would Pull Up the Ladder on Florida’s Small Businesses
item.description:
Washington didn’t build Florida’s 3.5 million small businesses. Low taxes, light regulation, and an entrepreneurial culture built an economy where 3.8 million Floridians...

The post AICOA Would Pull Up the Ladder on Florida’s Small Businesses appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions
item.description:
Lawmakers across the country often insist on making “investments” in economic programs to help fill a perceived need for skilled workers. But such efforts often put the needs of the state and favored industries ahead of the needs of individuals -- and they miss big picture questions in the process.  This critical view should be […]

The post UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF President discusses AI data centers on “Powering America” podcast
item.description:
In the latest episode of the Powering America Podcast, Paul Gessing, president of the Rio Grande Foundation, discusses data centers and energy policy in New Mexico. Gessing highlights the state’s potential for data center development due to its natural resources and stable environment, while also addressing the challenges posed by political opposition and environmental concerns. The […]

The post RGF President discusses AI data centers on “Powering America” podcast appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The False Promise of Portland’s “All-Electric” High Schools
item.description:
John A. Charles, Jr. Portland Public Schools (PPS) held a ground-breaking ceremony in...

The post The False Promise of Portland’s “All-Electric” High Schools appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine Taxpayers Are Once Again Paying for Bellows’ Fight with Trump
item.description:
After a controversial Bureau of Motor Vehicles decision, the Maine taxpayers are once again being dragged into a high-profile constitutional fight with the Trump administration. The U.S. Department of Justice has sued the State of Maine, Governor Janet Mills, Attorney General Aaron Frey, and Secretary of State Shenna Bellows over a Bureau of Motor Vehicles […]

The post Maine Taxpayers Are Once Again Paying for Bellows’ Fight with Trump appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation hails Louisiana’s new worker-protection bill
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today applauded the Louisiana Legislature for passing Senate Bill 312, a measure that strengthens worker freedom by ensuring employees understand their rights before joining a union. 

The post Freedom Foundation hails Louisiana’s new worker-protection bill appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Mspolicy] How Good is Your Kid's School? A Clarion Ledger Exclusive
item.description: The post How Good is Your Kid's School? A Clarion Ledger Exclusive appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The Bottom-Up Case for Regulating AI Chatbots
item.description:
When looking to regulate emerging technologies, the popular instinct has been to draft these rules from the top...

The post The Bottom-Up Case for Regulating AI Chatbots appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] The Climate Superfund Act Is the Wrong Approach for New Jersey
item.description:
by Wells Winegar, Executive Director   On Thursday, the Assembly Environment and Solid Waste Committee will consider A3735, the so-called […]

The post The Climate Superfund Act Is the Wrong Approach for New Jersey first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] First Principles Series: Don’t Ban Data Centers. Don’t Subsidize Them, Either.
item.description: We can allow data centers to exist while keeping the courts open for nuisance lawsuits against bad actors who infringe upon protections like water rights when other property owners are actually harmed by them.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Unconstitutional Programs and the Administrative State: Stopping the 1419 Project
item.description: Over the past century, political progressives have built a bureaucratic machine with the goal of undermining our republic. It accomplishes this by making people dependent on government programs and by ignoring the will of the people and their elected representatives, ruling solely through executive fiat. Idaho has long been at the forefront of the battle […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] BET cuts for the long run
item.description:
As the 2026 legislative session wound down, it looked as though Republicans wouldn’t be able to agree on one of the party’s signature issues: cutting business taxes. During committee of conference negotiations last week, word spread (and was even reported in the news) that a House-passed Business Enterprise Tax cut was dead.  House and Senate […]

The post BET cuts for the long run appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Un-Affordability: Speaker Blazejewski’s Budget Denies Science, Will Raise Energy Costs for Ocean Staters … but Center Claims One Major Victory
item.description:
Despite common-sense energy reforms suggested by Governor McKee; despite pledged reforms by all other New England states; and despite indisputable research that shows how Rhode Island’s net zero emissions energy strategy will lead to unaffordable costs and dangerous risks … the budget put forth by the new Speaker of the RI House of Representative, Christopher Blazejewski … doubles-down on the state’s radical Act On Climate mandates.

The post Un-Affordability: Speaker Blazejewski’s Budget Denies Science, Will Raise Energy Costs for Ocean Staters … but Center Claims One Major Victory appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Public school leaders look forward to possible private donations for scholarships
item.description:
Public school district leaders are thinking about what scholarships from private donations through a new federal tax credit program the will do for their students.

The post Public school leaders look forward to possible private donations for scholarships appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Property Tax Relief Options Explained
item.description: The most important point to understand regarding property taxes is that they are budget-driven. This simply means local taxing districts, which include counties, cities, schools, fire districts, etc., set property tax budgets, and those budgets are the primary driver of property tax payments and the changes (usually increases) over time. Long-term property tax relief requires […]

item.title: [Empirecenter] A Bullying Lawyer Trapped an Innocent Woman in a Five-Year Court Battle – but New York Law can’t Make her Whole
item.description:
We like to associate the law with justice—protecting the innocent and punishing the wrongdoers. But sometimes our laws punish the....

The post A Bullying Lawyer Trapped an Innocent Woman in a Five-Year Court Battle – but New York Law can’t Make her Whole appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Lawsuit challenges new Hawaii law that would silence civic advocacy
item.description:
The Institute for Free Speech has filed a lawsuit on behalf of the Grassroot Institute to challenge Act 11, which stands to block the nonprofit’s civic advocacy efforts HONOLULU, June 5, 2026 >> A sweeping new Hawaii law aims to silence the groups that residents most typically use to make their voices heard — including […]

The post Lawsuit challenges new Hawaii law that would silence civic advocacy appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Healthcare Highlights in the New State Budget
item.description:
Governor Hochul’s focus on affordability seems to have skipped over the healthcare portions of the new state budget. The deal....

The post Healthcare Highlights in the New State Budget appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Court Filing Revives Questions About Hartford Councilman and AFSCME Chief of Staff TJ Clarke
item.description: A new Hartford court filing is reviving scrutiny of City Council President Thomas “TJ” Clarke II and the city’s handling of a sexual harassment controversy that ended in a taxpayer-funded […]

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Coalition Of Child Safety Advocates Files Amicus Briefs Defending Texas App Store Accountability Act In Fifth Circuit
item.description:
AUSTIN, TX – A broad coalition of child safety advocates, policy organizations, and Texas nonprofits filed multiple amicus briefs urging the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit to reinstate Texas’ App Store Accountability Act and reverse an injunction blocking the law from taking effect.

The post Coalition Of Child Safety Advocates Files Amicus Briefs Defending Texas App Store Accountability Act In Fifth Circuit appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Casino in Kids’ Pockets: Why Senator Katie Britt is Right to Fight Gambling Ads
item.description:
Years ago, parents could lock their front doors at night and reasonably assume their children were safe from the world’s adult vices. Today, that security is an illusion. The dangers no longer wait at the doorstep; they bypass it entirely, slipping directly into our homes through the glowing screens in the palms of our children’s hands.

The post The Casino in Kids’ Pockets: Why Senator Katie Britt is Right to Fight Gambling Ads appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] More Money Won’t Fix Our Schools. Our Data Proves It
item.description:
For decades, America has been told that the key to better schools is more money. Underperformance, the argument runs, is really a question of resources. Just give the teacher unions what they ask for, and good outcomes will follow. Mississippi is starting to show that this simply is not true. Over the past decade, Mississippi […]

The post More Money Won’t Fix Our Schools. Our Data Proves It appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Circuit Court – Adams County
item.description:
Circuit Court – Adams County

The post Circuit Court – Adams County appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Coverage vs. Care (Part 1)
item.description: On Valentine’s Day 1966, Gov. Bob Smylie called a special session of the state Legislature to deal, primarily, with reapportioning the Idaho Senate to comply with the U.S. Supreme Court’s one-man-one-vote requirement. Back then, there was a senator for each of the state’s 44 counties, and the courts had ruled the arrangement unconstitutional. So when […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells’ $368,885 Secret Meeting
item.description:
For Immediate Release: June 10, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org   IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells $368,885 Secret Meeting Lawsuit argues DPI violated open meetings laws, seeks to invalidate DPI’s secret meeting that weakened Wisconsin testing standards Delafield, Wis. — The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG), represented by the Wisconsin Transparency Project, is taking the Department […]

The post IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells’ $368,885 Secret Meeting appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Fiscal Transparency Problem
item.description: Connecticut officials often present the state as a model of fiscal discipline, pointing to budget surpluses, pension payments, fiscal rules and bond-rating upgrades as evidence that Hartford has turned a […]

item.title: [Will Law] Swan v Rollins
item.description:
This lawsuit is brought by three Wisconsin dairy farmers forced to subsidize the Dairy Checkoff: a burdensome federal program that leads to the demise of small farms under the guise of environmental “sustainability.” Not only is this compelled subsidy Orwellian, but it also violates the First Amendment to the United States Constitution and the Administrative Procedure Act.

The post Swan v Rollins appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Jake Curtis | Point Taken
item.description: The post Jake Curtis | Point Taken appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Laura Ebke on Second Chances in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Platte Institute Senior Fellow Laura Ebke. During the episode Laura and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss a new short documentary from the Platte Institute highlighting the impact of the second chances legislation that was a part of LB 16 passed in 2024.  “Second Chance:...

The post Nebraskanomics: Laura Ebke on Second Chances in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Victory: Pittsville School District Admits Parent’s Social Media Post Was Protected Speech, Settles First Amendment Dispute
item.description:
WILL secured a settlement agreement with the School District of Pittsville to vindicate the First Amendment rights of Amanda Vogel, a parent who received litigation threats from school district officials and visits from law enforcement following a social media post.

The post Victory: Pittsville School District Admits Parent’s Social Media Post Was Protected Speech, Settles First Amendment Dispute appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansans Need Facts about Data Centers
item.description: My earlier Kansas Policy Institute piece, “Kansas Should Welcome AI Growth, Not Zone It Away”, drew a lot of feedback. Much of it came down to four concerns: water, electricity, zoning, and whether data centers really help local communities. Those are fair questions. Big projects often face scrutiny, and Kansans deserve answers about how new […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Ranks First in K-12 Education–But Not for Long
item.description:
Since 2005, Massachusetts has achieved the top ranking in the biannual National Assessment of Educational Progress’s (NAEP) fourth-grade reading assessment. However, the Commonwealth’s lower quality Common Core-based English Language Arts (ELA) standards and lack of quality of reading pedagogy and curriculum at the local level are coming back to haunt it.

The post Massachusetts Ranks First in K-12 Education–But Not for Long appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Food co-op seen as viable, more likely option than government-funded grocery store in Milwaukee
item.description:
The solution to problems left by grocery store closures in Milwaukee may include a civil-society option such as a food co-op, observers say.

The post Food co-op seen as viable, more likely option than government-funded grocery store in Milwaukee appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s 2026 Primary Shows Why RCV Should Be Repealed
item.description:
Maine voters went to the polls on June 9, but in several of the state’s most important primary races, Election Day did not actually produce a winner. Instead, both gubernatorial primaries and the Democratic primary in Maine’s 2nd Congressional District are headed into ranked-choice voting tabulation. As a result, Mainers are left waiting days after […]

The post Maine’s 2026 Primary Shows Why RCV Should Be Repealed appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Facts to help you decide whether Wisconsin children should be eligible for donor-funded education scholarships
item.description:
What Wisconsinites need to know about how the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program works.

The post Facts to help you decide whether Wisconsin children should be eligible for donor-funded education scholarships appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] How States Are Reshaping Income Tax Policy in 2026- Part 3
item.description:
Colorado's Initiative 195 would replace the state's 4.4% flat income tax with a graduated income tax and significantly increase the amount of revenue the state can retain and spend. The proposal represents one of the most significant challenges to Colorado's Taxpayer Bill of Rights (TABOR), a constitutional limit on taxes and spending that has returned more than $12 billion to taxpayers since 1992. The debate highlights a broader national divide over fiscal policy, pitting advocates of higher taxes and government spending against supporters of taxpayer protections, spending limits, and voter control over tax increases.

The post How States Are Reshaping Income Tax Policy in 2026- Part 3 appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Testimony on PPS’ Racial Educational Equity Policy (Policy 2.10.010-P)
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. June 8, 2026 RE: Policy 2.10.010-P Dear Board...

The post Testimony on PPS’ Racial Educational Equity Policy (Policy 2.10.010-P) appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] PPS’ Failed Equity Policy: No Evidence, No Results
item.description:
By Naomi InmanTestimony by John A. Charles, Jr. Portland Public Schools briefly revisited...

The post PPS’ Failed Equity Policy: No Evidence, No Results appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Welfare Cards Used in Hawaii, Guam and Across the Country, Records Show
item.description: Connecticut electronic benefit cards (EBT) were used in more than $7.2 million in taxpayer-funded cash transactions outside the state over a roughly two-and-a-half-year period, including in Florida resort towns, Hawaii […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] Does Jeff Bezos benefit from a NH Business Enterprise Tax cut?
item.description:
New Hampshire’s unique state tax structure is hard to understand, especially for people who don’t own a business.  More than a third (36%) of the state General Fund and Education Trust Fund revenue comes from business taxes.  The vast majority of that comes from the Business Profits Tax (BPT), but the lesser-known Business Enterprise Tax […]

The post Does Jeff Bezos benefit from a NH Business Enterprise Tax cut? appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] SPECIAL FEATURE: Louisiana’s School Funding Reckoning: Teacher Pay, the MFP, and the Need for a Bigger Conversation
item.description:
Louisiana’s debate over teacher pay has exposed a deeper problem that policymakers can no longer afford to ignore: the state’s public education funding system is increasingly disconnected from student enrollment, taxpayer expectations, and the educational needs of Louisiana’s children and their families. Governor Jeff Landry’s recent executive order directing the redirection of $168 million within...

The post SPECIAL FEATURE: Louisiana’s School Funding Reckoning: Teacher Pay, the MFP, and the Need for a Bigger Conversation appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Taxing Away Success
item.description:
We are witnessing a troubling migration—a flight of capital and talent from states that have adopted increasingly aggressive tax policies. Elon Musk moved Tesla and X from California to Texas; Palantir relocated to Colorado. High-profile individuals and corporations are pulling up stakes and relocating to states with more favorable tax policies. A casual observer might...

The post Taxing Away Success first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Bear Flag and California’s history blind spot
item.description:
June 14 is the anniversary of the 1846 Bear Flag Revolt that helped set California on the path to statehood. But how many California students could explain what the Bear Flag Republic was, why it mattered, or how California became a state? The truth is, we have no idea. California schools are expected to teach...

The post The Bear Flag and California’s history blind spot first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] How Many Laws Are Too Many?
item.description:
A total of 1,259 bills were filed during the 2026 Louisiana Legislative Session, which adjourned on June 1, and hundreds have already been signed into law. That should give pause to Louisianans with a healthy skepticism of government.  To be sure, some new laws improve liberty by removing outdated restrictions or undoing burdensome regulations. SB...

The post How Many Laws Are Too Many? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Defending the First Amendment
item.description:
Free speech is one of our country’s founding principles. But that doesn’t mean it is safe from attack. Just this past month, the Hawaiʻi Legislature passed a bill — which the governor promptly signed into law —  that will directly infringe on our First Amendment rights when it goes into effect in July 2027. That new […]

The post Defending the First Amendment appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Alternative Families by the Numbers THE GOVERNOR is getting grief over declaring June “Nuclear Family Month.” Does he think there’s something wrong with non-nuclear families? Well, there’s a two-part answer to that, and some aren’t going to like the second part. First, let’s acknowledging that raising a family, any family, is a heroic endeavor. Raising a family as a single Read the full article...

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Wealthiest taxpayers pay more than their fair share of income taxes
item.description: I recently was talking with someone who said wealthy people should pay their fair share of income taxes, and I asked, “What do you mean by ‘fair share ‘?” He struggled with the question, so I said, “Do you mean that a group of people with 50% of all the income should pay 50% of […]

item.title: [Empirecenter] It Is Time to Rethink the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative
item.description:
Before budget negotiations, Gov. Hochul warned that unless New York changes its climate plans, New Yorkers could face a $2.26-per-gallon....

The post It Is Time to Rethink the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Data centers aren’t draining the Mountain West
item.description: Data centers have the potential to be an economic boon to the regions in which they are built, along with laying the groundwork for advances in medicine, national defense, and other research. Slowing data center development must be done based on local context, including the consumption levels of the data center and regional water availability.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Report on economic development incentives is incomplete
item.description:
The following appeared in the Santa Fe New Mexican on June 6, 2026. It appeared in other papers across the state. Recently the Legislative Finance Committee produced a report in which (to summarize the findings) they made the case that New Mexico “spends” over half-a-billion dollars on tax breaks that generated little economic return for […]

The post Opinion piece: Report on economic development incentives is incomplete appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Arizona voters to decide on measure curtailing taxpayer support for teachers’ unions
item.description:
The Arizona Senate today passed House Concurrent Resolution 2040, a Freedom Foundation-backed measure that would prohibit public school districts from using taxpayer resources to support union organizing activities.

The post Arizona voters to decide on measure curtailing taxpayer support for teachers’ unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Free Markets, Local Control, and the Impact of Minimum Wage Policy on Rural Nebraska
item.description:
Nebraska hit a major wage milestone in January 2026 when the state’s minimum wage rose to $15 an hour, the final step in a voter-approved 2022 plan. But the debate did not end there.  Soon after, the Legislature passed LB 258. The law replaced inflation-based increases with a fixed 1.75% annual increase starting in 2027....

The post Free Markets, Local Control, and the Impact of Minimum Wage Policy on Rural Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] API Launches Grassroots Push Urging Trump Administration to End the “Mail-Order Abortion Crime Scene” and Restore the In-Person Doctor’s Visit for Chemical Abortion Pills
item.description:
Alabama Policy Institute launched a grassroots effort calling on the Trump administration and members of Congress to end Joe Biden’s dangerous mail-order abortion scheme and restore the in-person doctor’s visit requirement that must be met before a woman can obtain chemical abortion drugs.

The post API Launches Grassroots Push Urging Trump Administration to End the “Mail-Order Abortion Crime Scene” and Restore the In-Person Doctor’s Visit for Chemical Abortion Pills appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan roads improve
item.description: Repairs on track to keep up with wear and tear

item.title: [Inpolicy] Eichenberger: Father’s Day
item.description: by Dan Eichenberger, M.D. With Father’s Day approaching, the cultural conversation about men, fathers, and masculinity deserves more care than slogans usually allow. This is a season when many families pause to honor fathers, grandfathers, mentors and father figures whose steady presence has shaped lives in quiet but lasting ways. For that reason, the way society Read the full article...

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] What is the Declaration of Independence?
item.description: Most of us understand that the Constitution is a statement of positive law—that is, man-made law—but ask whether the Declaration is law as well.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] AZ Constitutional Amendment Will Block Attack on Military Families
item.description:
In a major victory for Arizonans, the state legislature has sent to the ballot the Goldwater Institute’s Military Family Protection..

The post AZ Constitutional Amendment Will Block Attack on Military Families appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Risk, Reform, and Public Safety in Missouri with Doug Burris
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Doug Burris, retired Justice Services Director and Chief United States Probation Officer, about criminal justice reform in Missouri and St. Louis. They discuss Missouri’s risk-based […]

The post Risk, Reform, and Public Safety in Missouri with Doug Burris appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Which States Have Greatest Fiscal Resilience?
item.description:
A new Fiscal Resilience Index ranked Iowa 8th nationally, reflecting the state's strong fiscal position and ability to withstand potential disruptions in federal funding. The study warns that states have become increasingly dependent on federal dollars, leaving them vulnerable to Washington's budget instability, policy changes, and growing national debt. Iowa's combination of spending restraint, strong reserves, low liabilities, and sound fiscal management demonstrates how conservative fiscal policies can strengthen both state finances and state independence. “State governments today operate within a fiscal environment that is increasingly shaped by decisions made in Washington.”

The post Which States Have Greatest Fiscal Resilience? appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] THE WALZ-ELLISON REPORT: What they KNEW and WHEN
item.description:
WHAT Walz and Ellison knew about the FRAUD and WHEN they knew it…. Check out the American Experiment Podcast! A damning report from the U.S. House says Walz and Ellison knew about the fraud and did nothing, Minnesota’s national education ranking fell even further (somehow), and a murder in Mankato points to bigger problems with...

The post THE WALZ-ELLISON REPORT: What they KNEW and WHEN appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Standardize Maui County grant applications to improve transparency
item.description:
June 16, 2026, 9 a.m. Kalana O Maui Building To: Maui County Council Budget, Finance and Economic Development Committee Yuki Lei K. Sugimura, Chair Kauanoe Batangan, Vice Chair From: Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst Grassroot Institute of Hawaii RE: Bill 73 (2026) — ON REVIEWING THE MAUI COUNTY GRANTS PROGRAM Aloha Chair Sugimura, Vice Chair Batangan […]

The post Standardize Maui County grant applications to improve transparency appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] For the Children? Data Center Strengthens Local School District, Community
item.description:
Richland Parish, Louisiana is not often the subject of national news. This month has been an exception. The Wall Street Journal, among other high-profile media outlets, reported on “teachers getting $50,000 bonuses thanks to a massive meta data center.” The astonishing numbers don’t stop with the teacher bonuses. The Journal went on to report, “The...

The post For the Children? Data Center Strengthens Local School District, Community appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Washington’s Attorney General offers strong defense of signature gatherers’ rights
item.description: Amidst evidence of signature gatherers being harassed, Washington State Attorney General Nick Brown is offering support to prosecute those trying to interfere with the initiative and referendum process. This is a refreshing and much-needed defense of the people’s sacred constitutional rights and the rule of law.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] No, Kansas City Cannot Rush Royals Financing to Beat a Petition Vote
item.description:
Kansas City Mayor Lucas told KCMO Talk Radio on Thursday morning that, basically, the city is going to ignore the petitions submitted with 4,500 signatures to the city clerk requiring […]

The post No, Kansas City Cannot Rush Royals Financing to Beat a Petition Vote appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change
item.description:
Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change For years, Empower Mississippi has focused on removing external barriers to opportunity. But what if some of the barriers holding people back aren’t external at all? In this episode, Grant sits down with licensed professional counselor Dr. Brandon Henry to explore the internal obstacles that often prevent people from…

The post Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Has a Small-Home Shortage; Zoning Reform Can Help Fix It
item.description:
Boston – A new Pioneer Institute report finds that a mismatch between who lives in Massachusetts and the housing being built is squeezing two generations at once: young families who cannot get into a house, and seniors who cannot downsize.

Local land use rules often make it difficult to build small, reasonably priced, and low-maintenance homes, despite a growing need for them as the population ages and average household sizes continue to shrink.

The post Massachusetts Has a Small-Home Shortage; Zoning Reform Can Help Fix It appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] In Michigan, ‘free’ is expensive
item.description: Rich kids eat free, and you pay for it

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Vallas: Pritzker signs a budget giving Illinois tax hikes, higher spending
item.description:
Eight years of the governor’s budgets have exposed the cost of spending-driven budgeting and repeated tax hikes.

The post Vallas: Pritzker signs a budget giving Illinois tax hikes, higher spending appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Fraud of Official Art “Whenever I hear the word ‘culture,’ I reach for my Lugar.” —  Hermann Göring THE MAYOR this week unveiled “The Garner,” our city’s latest acquisition of outdoor art. It is shown above so you can judge for yourself but let me say I find it hopelessly abstruse. It took even the artist himself, Sacho Primo, Read the full article...

item.title: [Texaspolicy] While Mexico defends ‘sovereignty,’ cartels import a flesh-eating parasite into Texas
item.description:
Cartels move an estimated 800,000 cattle per year from Central America into Mexico using fake ear tags and falsified records. As the New World screwworm returns to American soil for the first time since its eradication 60 years ago, Texas is now on the front line of Mexico’s threat to U.S. sovereignty and national security. The return...

The post While Mexico defends ‘sovereignty,’ cartels import a flesh-eating parasite into Texas first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Mark McHargue on Nebraska’s Agriculture Industry
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome President of the Nebraska Farm Bureau, Mark McHargue. During the episode Mark and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the impact of agriculture in Nebraska, next steps on property tax reform, and what the future looks like for our state’s largest industry.  Listen now on...

The post Nebraskanomics: Mark McHargue on Nebraska’s Agriculture Industry appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] New Report: America Can Power the AI Boom, But It Needs to Build Fast Enough
item.description:
The artificial intelligence boom may sound like a story about software, algorithms, and the cloud—something ethereal that we cannot touch...

The post New Report: America Can Power the AI Boom, But It Needs to Build Fast Enough appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] MN funded nuke study, the devil is in the detail
item.description:
Hidden away on pages 43 to 45 of a 58 page MN state bill about taxes and finances, is a section entitled “Nuclear Energy Study; Appropriation.” The section is number 11. The bill is HF2438. It was signed into law by Governor Tim Walz, less than a month ago, on May 27th. As reported on...

The post MN funded nuke study, the devil is in the detail appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Rural Hospitals Need Prosperity
item.description: Kansas now has more rural hospitals at immediate risk of closure than any other state, according to KAKE News. That is serious. Rural healthcare access matters. Families should not have to drive for hours to access emergency care. But if Kansas treats this only as a hospital funding problem, it will miss the real issue. […]

item.title: [Pacificresearch] San Francisco’s Recovery—and Its Lessons
item.description:
A few years ago, I was one of San Francisco’s harshest critics. Writing for the Pacific Research Institute, I documented rising crime, retail theft, open-air drug markets, and a criminal justice philosophy that often seemed more focused on reducing incarceration and prosecution than on protecting public safety. During recalled district attorney Chesa Boudin’s years in […]

The post San Francisco’s Recovery—and Its Lessons appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Has a New Graduation Framework. It Still Needs a Stronger Accountability System
item.description:
Today's ruling is a serious disappointment for Massachusetts families struggling under one of the highest tax and cost burdens in the nation. The Court's decision rests on a drafting error in the Attorney General's summary of the petition—a matter entirely outside the control of voters and petition signers. As a result, the people of Massachusetts will be denied the opportunity to decide whether the Commonwealth should reduce the income tax rate from 5 percent to 4 percent.

The post Massachusetts Has a New Graduation Framework. It Still Needs a Stronger Accountability System appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] How Much Flow Preserves the Delta?
item.description:
When choosing what policies and projects might best ensure abundant water for California’s ecosystems, farms, and cities, the role of the delta is central. Even if large scale desalination were to someday deliver over a million acre feet of water per year to California’s coastal cities, how we manage the delta affects many times that...

The post How Much Flow Preserves the Delta? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Sues Over Illegal Giveaway to American Airlines
item.description:
When city leaders in Columbia, Mo., struck a deal with American Airlines to launch a new daily route to North..

The post Goldwater Sues Over Illegal Giveaway to American Airlines appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] A Pyrrhic Bargain on AI Preemption
item.description:
The White House and Congress are reportedly working on a deal to trade free expression online for free...

The post A Pyrrhic Bargain on AI Preemption appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] What motivates families to switch schools?
item.description:
I switched schools a handful of times throughout my K-12 education, and each time my parents had a very specific reason for doing so. Looking back, those experiences have made me curious about what drives other families to make the same choice. Switching schools is not an easy decision, at least it wasn’t for my...

The post What motivates families to switch schools? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] This Is Why Missouri Families Need Choice
item.description:
At the Show-Me Institute, we spend a lot of time analyzing the systemic, high-level benefits of educational freedom—how it spurs competition, how it affects state funding formulas, and how it […]

The post This Is Why Missouri Families Need Choice appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Canada’s Assisted-Suicide Boom Reveals the Human Costs of Single-Payer
item.description:
In Canada, access to medical care can take months. Access to assisted suicide, however, is much easier to come by. Ten years ago today, Canada’s Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID) program was legalized, and this year, Canada is on track to surpass 100,000 assisted-suicide deaths. Earlier this year, an Ontario physician met a 45-year-old man […]

The post Canada’s Assisted-Suicide Boom Reveals the Human Costs of Single-Payer appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory
item.description:
This post will be a  lot less policy oriented than normal. That’s because as the head of RGF, this week has been VERY busy. It started with a HUGE win at ABQ City Council on Monday night when on a 5-4 vote Council (again) rejected a major tax increase. The vote was 5-4 against raising […]

The post RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Spanberger boosts trans activist after avoiding topic during campaign
item.description:
Jessica Schwalb of the Washington Free Beacon highlights the latest evidence that Virginia’s governor is no moderate. “Moderate” Virginia governor Abigail Spanberger (D.) tapped a left-wing activist with a long history of pushing gender transitions for minors to serve on the state’s LGBTQ+ Advisory Board. As Equality Arlington president and founder, Kellen MacBeth has also...

The post Spanberger boosts trans activist after avoiding topic during campaign appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Media blame conservatives again for left-wing violence
item.description:
Brianna Lyman writes for the Federalist about another case of legacy media malfeasance. When a burning cross was found at a Chicago park last week, the media and Democrats already apparently knew who was responsible. “A large burning cross — a historic symbol of hate and intimidation against Black Americans — was discovered in a...

The post Media blame conservatives again for left-wing violence appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Left-winger supporting neo-Nazis may have had romantic link
item.description:
Chadwick Moore and Isabel Vincent report a significant development in the investigation against the Southern Poverty Law Center. A top Southern Poverty Law Center official is accused of helping funnel $1.2 million in donor money to an informant in the National Alliance white supremacist group — who was also allegedly her lover. The Department of...

The post Left-winger supporting neo-Nazis may have had romantic link appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] A 'Champion of Education?' Whitmer’s record tells a different story
item.description: State spends more while achievement declines

item.title: [Pacificresearch] The Trump administration tries to kill aid to dependent cities
item.description:
You know about the bridge to nowhere. The electric tugboat? Probably not. In 2020, the San Diego County Air Pollution Control District received a unique grant from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. Awarded under the Diesel Emissions Reduction Act (DERA), the $2,017,660 subsidy paid for the eWolf, “America’s first all-electric ship assist harbor tugboat.” How […]

The post The Trump administration tries to kill aid to dependent cities appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Wisconsin PTA v. Wisconsin State Assembly
item.description:
WILL represents a group of Wisconsin taxpayers & parents in a motion to intervene in Wisconsin PTA v. Wisconsin State Assembly. The complaint in that case seeks to have the judiciary determine the level and distribution of funding for education in this State rather than leave those decisions to the Legislature and seeks to reduce or eliminate Wisconsin’s School Choice Programs.

The post Wisconsin PTA v. Wisconsin State Assembly appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Pritzker gets his school cell phone ban bill despite CTU opposition
item.description:
The issue was a priority for the governor. Here’s what you need to know.

The post Pritzker gets his school cell phone ban bill despite CTU opposition appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Over 40% of Illinois bills passed in last week of session
item.description:
Giving Illinoisans a sense of déjà vu, the Illinois General Assembly raced the clock to pass legislation toward the final day of session — or in some cases just after the deadline.

The post Over 40% of Illinois bills passed in last week of session appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Rural Voices Still Matter
item.description:
Federalism was designed to ensure that every state has a voice, not just the nation’s largest population centers. The Electoral College helps preserve that balance by encouraging presidential candidates to build support across diverse regions, including rural America. As power becomes increasingly centralized in Washington, protecting federalism remains essential to preserving local self-government, state sovereignty, and meaningful representation for rural communities.

The post Rural Voices Still Matter appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] We’re Optimizing Our Way Out Of Love
item.description:
Recently, I was out with friends and I met a guy. Nothing serious, just a little flirting. He was cute, I’m single, and as I said goodbye, he handed me his phone with a new contact open. I typed in my number. Fifteen minutes later, while I was driving home, he texted. Something vague and...

The post We’re Optimizing Our Way Out Of Love first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Congress should follow the Texas approach to child safety online
item.description:
Big Tech is fighting this tooth and nail. But it’s the right thing. Few things have been more troubling in recent years than the unfettered social experiment of throwing children in front of black mirrors and hopping them up on app-induced dopamine. After a decade of witnessing the hollowing out of their children firsthand, parents...

The post Congress should follow the Texas approach to child safety online first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.
